title: Scanline — a software 3D renderer
description: A software 3D renderer AND a from-scratch path tracer for the same scene — no WebGL, no GPU, every pixel written by hand in TypeScript. The RASTERIZER transforms, near-plane clips (Sutherland–Hodgman), perspective-divides and scan-converts each triangle with perspective-correct interpolation, a z-buffer, backface culling, two lighting models (Blinn–Phong and a metallic-roughness Cook–Torrance PBR path — GGX/Smith/Fresnel) lit by an analytic HDR environment (skybox + diffuse irradiance + roughness-blurred specular), tangent-space normal mapping, real-time PCF shadow mapping, and an HDR post pipeline (bloom, ACES/Reinhard/Filmic tone mapping, FXAA, vignette). NEW — a RAY-TRACED REFERENCE PATH ('software RTX'): the identical scene is flattened into a world-space triangle soup, indexed by a from-scratch binned-SAH BOUNDING VOLUME HIERARCHY, and rendered by a Monte-Carlo PATH TRACER that shares the rasterizer's exact BSDF — Möller–Trumbore intersection, next-event estimation to punctual AND emissive-area lights, multi-bounce global illumination with colour bleeding, soft (cone/area-sampled) shadows, true mirror & glossy inter-reflection, Russian-roulette termination and the analytic sky as an infinite emitter. It refines PROGRESSIVELY while the camera is still (free anti-aliasing via jittered camera rays) and a draggable SPLIT-SCREEN puts the real-time rasterizer next to the ground-truth path tracer so the shadow-map vs ray-traced-shadow and IBL-probe vs true-reflection differences sit side by side. Plus a pure ambient-occlusion mode, a Cornell box and a hall-of-mirrors scene that only read correctly under GI, an in-app numerical VERIFICATION suite (Möller–Trumbore vs analytic, BVH vs brute force, the sampling statistics, and a furnace energy-conservation test), paste-import of any Wavefront OBJ, PNG export, eight rasterizer debug views, an orbit camera and live stats. NEWEST — a DEFERRED G-BUFFER + SCREEN-SPACE GLOBAL ILLUMINATION pillar that closes the gap between the real-time raster path and the ray-traced reference: the shaded pass records world position/normal/albedo/material plus the lighting split (direct/diffuse-IBL/specular-IBL), then hand-written screen-space passes resolve the indirect light — SSAO (view-space hemisphere kernel), SCREEN-SPACE REFLECTIONS (depth-march that replaces the IBL probe by the real on-screen reflection, so the floor and metals reflect each other), and contact shadows — followed by TEMPORAL ANTI-ALIASING (Halton jitter + reprojection + neighbourhood clamp) that supersamples a still frame for free. Four new deferred debug views (position/roughness/ambient-occlusion/reflections), an Interior showcase scene, and a headless+in-app numerical self-test suite for the screen-space passes.
